story.iWant: to rollback to a previous theme version
story.soThat: I can undo unwanted changes and restore a known-good theme state

tasks.task: Implement Version Manager - List Versions 1.1: Scan `.slide-builder/theme-history/` for `theme-v*.json` files 1.2: Parse version number and date from each filename using regex 1.3: Sort versions by version number (ascending) 1.4: Return formatted list: `[{ version: "1", date: "2026-01-26", path: "..." }, ...]` 1.5: Handle empty directory (return empty list with message) 1.6: Load each version's meta.changeNotes for description if available

Implement Version Selection and Validation 2.1: Parse user input for version number (support "1", "v1", "previous") 2.2: "previous" keyword resolves to N-1 where N is current version 2.3: Validate version exists in theme-history/ 2.4: If invalid, display error with available version numbers 2.5: Load selected version file and parse JSON 2.6: Verify JSON structure is valid theme schema

Implement Sample Deck Preview 3.1: Load selected version's theme.json content 3.2: Regenerate 6 sample slides using frontend-design skill 3.3: Save samples to `.slide-builder/samples/` 3.4: Open samples in browser for user preview 3.5: Display message: "Sample deck regenerated with v{N} theme. Review in browser."

Implement Confirmation Prompt 4.1: After preview, display confirmation prompt 4.2: Show: "Restore v{N} from {date}? Current theme will be preserved as v{M}. (y/n)" 4.3: Accept "y", "yes", "n", "no", "cancel" as valid responses 4.4: If "n" or "cancel", exit workflow without changes 4.5: If "y", proceed to restore

Implement Current State Preservation 5.1: Before restore, read current theme.json 5.2: Determine next version number (N+1) 5.3: Save current theme to `theme-history/theme-v{N+1}-{date}.json` 5.4: Log to status.yaml: "Theme v{N+1} saved to history (pre-rollback backup)" 5.5: Verify backup file exists and is valid JSON

Implement Theme Restoration 6.1: Copy selected version content to theme.json 6.2: Update meta.version to new number (N+2, not original version number) 6.3: Update meta.lastModified to current timestamp (ISO 8601) 6.4: Add meta.changeNotes: "Rolled back from v{N+1} to v{X} settings" 6.5: Save updated theme.json 6.6: Verify save succeeded

Implement Template Regeneration 7.1: Compare restored theme shapes/layouts with previous theme 7.2: If shape primitives differ, regenerate layout templates 7.3: If only colors/typography differ, skip template regeneration 7.4: Log template regeneration status to status.yaml

Implement Status Logger for Rollback 8.1: Log "Theme rolled back to v{X}" to status.yaml last_action 8.2: Update last_modified timestamp 8.3: Append to history array with rollback details 8.4: Display success message with version details

Integrate with /theme-edit Workflow 9.1: Review theme-edit/instructions.md for rollback trigger 9.2: Add rollback detection: if feedback contains "rollback" or "restore" 9.3: Route to rollback flow instead of edit flow 9.4: Support syntax: "rollback to v1", "restore version 2", "rollback to previous"

Testing - Rollback Verification 10.1: Create 3 versions through setup + 2 edits 10.2: Request rollback, verify list shows v1, v2, v3 with dates 10.3: Select v1, verify samples regenerated with v1 theme 10.4: Confirm rollback and verify state preservation 10.5: Request invalid version "v99", verify error with available list 10.6: Test "previous" keyword (rollback to N-1) 10.7: Verify templates regenerated if shapes changed 10.8: Verify status.yaml history updated 10.9: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given theme history exists with multiple versions, when the user requests rollback, then available versions are listed
Each version in the list shows version number and date
User can select a specific version to restore
Sample slides are regenerated with the selected version's theme for preview
User must confirm before rollback is applied
The current theme state is saved as a new version BEFORE rollback (preserving current state)
The rolled-back state becomes a new version number (not overwriting history)
Given an invalid version number is requested, then error shows available versions
Templates are regenerated after rollback if needed

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines AC6.4.1-AC6.4.9, Version Manager restore operation, Theme Rollback Workflow phases, and rollback safety pattern ensuring no data loss.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Defines .slide-builder/ directory structure including theme-history/, status.yaml schema with history array, and ISO 8601 timestamp format.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: "Users can rollback to previous theme version" - core functional requirement for theme version history feature.

doc.title: Epics and Stories
doc.snippet: Story 6.4 Theme Rollback user story with high-level AC definitions and context for version history management.

doc.title: Previous Story: Theme Version History
doc.snippet: Establishes Version Manager foundation including theme-history/ directory management, version save logic, and naming pattern theme-v{N}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json.

doc.title: Story 6.2: Theme Editing
doc.snippet: Established /theme-edit workflow structure with feedback loop pattern, sample regeneration using frontend-design skill, and approval flow.

file.reason: Primary integration point for rollback - need to add rollback detection when user provides "rollback" or "restore" feedback. Contains existing Phase 1 version save logic (lines 66-73), sample regeneration pattern (Phase 4), and status logging patterns.
Phase 2.5 contains version list display logic - scan theme-history/, parse filenames, sort by version. Reuse this pattern for rollback version listing.
Current theme structure showing version "2.0", meta.changeNotes array, shapes primitives, and all theme schema fields. Target for rollback restore operation.
Example version file showing original v1.0 theme structure. Used as source for rollback restore - compare shapes (borderRadius 8px vs 0px) to determine if templates need regeneration.
State file to update with rollback actions. Contains history array pattern, last_action field, last_modified timestamp, and theme.status field.
Sample slide for preview - regenerated with selected version theme during rollback preview phase.
Sample deck index - opens in browser for user to preview theme during rollback selection.
Layout templates directory - regenerated if shape primitives change during rollback.

dependencies.note: This project is a BMAD agentic framework for Claude Code - not a traditional npm/pip project. Dependencies are Claude Code skills and workflows, not packages.
dependencies.skill: Used for regenerating 6 sample slides during rollback preview
Primary command that will route to rollback flow
Displays theme with version history summary

constraints.constraint: NEVER delete version history entries - all versions must be preserved for audit trail
Current theme state MUST be saved BEFORE restore operation - no data loss scenario possible
Rollback creates NEW version entry (e.g., v4 might be rollback to v2 settings) - don't overwrite original version number
Full rollback operation: < 30 seconds (includes sample regeneration per NFR)
Templates regenerated ONLY if shape primitives change (borderRadius, shadow, arrow styles) - not for color/typography changes
All timestamps in ISO 8601 format per status.yaml patterns
Status logger updates: last_action, last_modified, history array append
User must confirm rollback after previewing samples - no automatic restore

interface.name: Version Manager - List Versions
interface.signature: listVersions(): Array<{ version: string, date: string, path: string, changeNotes?: string }>

interface.name: Version Manager - Restore
interface.signature: restoreVersion(versionNumber: string): { restoredTheme: ThemeJson, error: string | null }
interface.path: NEW - to be implemented in theme-edit/instructions.md

interface.name: Theme Editor - Sample Regeneration
interface.signature: regenerateSamples(theme: ThemeJson): void // Generates 6 slides to .slide-builder/samples/ using frontend-design skill

interface.name: Status Logger
interface.signature: updateStatus(action: string, additionalFields?: object): void // Updates last_action, last_modified, appends to history

interface.name: Template Updater
interface.signature: updateTemplatesIfNeeded(oldTheme: ThemeJson, newTheme: ThemeJson): void // Compares shapes, regenerates templates if changed

tests.standards: This is an agentic workflow project - testing is manual via Frontend Test Gates. Each story includes a test gate checklist with prerequisites, test steps, success criteria, and feedback questions. Tests verify workflow behavior by running commands and checking file system state.

ideas.idea: Verify version list displays all theme-v*.json files from theme-history/ with correct version numbers and dates parsed from filenames
Test version selection with formats: "1", "v1", "previous" - all should resolve correctly
After version selection, verify 6 sample slides regenerated in samples/ with selected version's theme colors and shapes applied
Verify confirmation prompt appears and "n"/"cancel" exits without changes, "y" proceeds
Before rollback completion, verify current theme saved to theme-history/ with incremented version number
After rollback, verify theme.json has restored content but NEW version number (not original v1 number)
Test invalid version "v99" - should display error with list of available versions
Compare v1 (borderRadius 8px) vs v2 (borderRadius 0px) - rollback from v2 to v1 should trigger template regeneration
Run full Frontend Test Gate 6-4-TG1 checklist to verify end-to-end workflow
